name: Fertilization Process
description: Fertilization Process in Animals. This process represents a higher-level biological mechanism distinct from molecular/biochemical processes covered in GLMP.
mermaid:
graph TD
    A2[Sperm Release] --> B2[Sperm Migration]
    C2[Ovum Maturation] --> D2[Ovulation]
    B2 --> E2[Zona Pellucida Binding]
    D2 --> E2
    E2 --> F2[Acrosome Reaction]
    F2 --> G2[Enzyme Release]
    G2 --> H2[Zona Penetration]
    H2 --> I2[Membrane Fusion]
    I2 --> J2[Cortical Reaction]
    J2 --> K2[Polyspermy Block]
    K2 --> L2[Pronuclei Formation]
    L2 --> M2[Zygote Formation]
    
    style A2 fill:#ff6b6b,color:#fff
    style C2 fill:#ff6b6b,color:#fff
    style B2 fill:#ffd43b,color:#000
    style D2 fill:#ffd43b,color:#000
    style E2 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style F2 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style G2 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style H2 fill:#74c0fc,color:#fff
    style I2 fill:#74c0fc,color:#fff
    style J2 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style K2 fill:#74c0fc,color:#fff
    style L2 fill:#74c0fc,color:#fff
    style M2 fill:#b197fc,color:#fff

sources.title: Programming Framework: A Universal Process Visualization Methodology
sources.notes: Higher-level biological process visualization: Fertilization Process. Created using the Programming Framework methodology for organismal and ecological process analysis.

sources.title: The Mammalian Fertilization Cascade: From Sperm-Egg Recognition to Zygote Formation
The molecular biology of mammalian fertilization
sources.authors: Wassarman, P.M., Liu, D.J., Qi, H., Wu, H., and Kligman, J.M.

sources.title: Local and global calcium signals during fertilization
sources.authors: Deguchi, R., Fukami, K., and Miyazaki, S.

notes: Higher-level biological process. GLMP covers biochemical/molecular processes; this database covers organismal/ecological processes.
